items.title: shell-quote quote() does not escape newlines in object .op values
items.description:
### Summary

`shell-quote`'s `quote()` function did not validate object-token inputs against the operator model used by `parse()`. The `.op` field was backslash-escaped character by character using `/(.)/g`, which in JavaScript does not match line terminators (`\n`, `\r`, U+2028, U+2029). A line terminator in `.op` therefore passed through unescaped into the output; POSIX shells treat a literal `\n` as a command separator, so any content after it would execute as a second command.

The vulnerable code path is reachable in two ways. Neither requires the parser to misbehave — `parse()` only emits ops from a fixed control set — but both are documented API surface:

1. **Direct construction.** A caller builds `{ op: '...\n...' }` from external input (e.g. a deserialized argument array) and passes it to `quote()`.
2. **`envFn` return.** `parse(cmd, envFn)` is documented to splice the return value of `envFn` into the result array when it is an object. An attacker-influenced data source consu

items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — Google Chrome 0-Day Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild  Update Now
id: 16c2a4bd-024b-507e-914b-5c61df6aff7b
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to Google Chrome 0-Day Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild  Update Now (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: CRITICAL | Class: GENERIC
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/Google Chrome 0-Day Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild  Update Now
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/06/09
modified: 2026/06/09
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: critical
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — Google Chrome 0-Day Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild  Update Now — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-06-09T15:59:09Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "Google Chrome 0-Day Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild  Update Now", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX Google Chrome 0-Day Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild  Update Now Exploitation Attempt - GENERIC"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:7455443; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-06-09, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)

items.title: nebula-mesh: API endpoints lack ownership checks, enabling cross-operator privilege escalation
items.description:
The `/api/v1/*` route surface trusts the bearer token alone for authorisation on most endpoints. The codebase itself admits this at `internal/api/hosts.go:384`: *"API trusts the bearer token for authorisation; per-CA ownership is enforced only in the Web layer."*

The Web UI gates state-changing routes through `loadAccessibleCA` (`internal/web/cas.go`); CA-management endpoints in `internal/api/cas.go` ALSO have proper `canAccessCA` gates. **The gap is on the host, network, firewall, mobile-bundle, and most operator endpoints.** Combined with the per-operator CA model from ADR 0002, this gives any non-admin operator API key broad cross-tenant access — instant privilege escalation in the worst case.

## Affected
All released versions prior to v0.3.4.

## Exploit chain

### A) Mint admin API key from any operator key (instant privilege escalation)
`internal/api/operators.go:118` — `handleCreateOperatorAPIKey` does no admin check and no actor/target-operator ownership check. Any operator k
